complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Fix: Make filters accessible in feed empty state (prevent user trap with zero results)
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down this UI refactoring into subtasks: (1) Refactor empty state rendering to conditionally show filtered-empty vs DB-empty states based on active_filter_count, (2) Hoist filter controls (floating button + FilterSheet) outside conditional branches to make them accessible from all non-error states, (3) Verification with TypeScript, lint, and manual smoke testing on both empty state scenarios. Each subtask should document the specific lines changed and verification results.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low complexity (3/10) because: (1) Single-file change to FeedScreen.tsx with clear implementation path already documented, (2) All dependencies exist - EmptyState component supports all needed props, filterStore exposes clear_filters() and active_filter_count, (3) No business logic changes - purely conditional UI rendering, (4) Non-critical path per CLAUDE.md §5 (screen component, not lib/hooks/utils), requiring only light verification (tsc + lint + smoke), (5) Well-defined acceptance criteria with no ambiguity. The task is essentially moving ~30 lines of JSX and adding a conditional branch. Risk is minimal as existing components are reused without modification.
